{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Human Resource Personnel",
  "description": "Hotel Capitol is a modern and contemporary hotel that offers not only luxury but comfort at its best. The three-star hotel is strategically located at the heart of Omole Phase 1, making it easily accessible with excellent road networks to other parts of the mainland and the city of Lagos as a whole. The 32-room Hotel offers tastefully furnished deluxe executive and super executive rooms as well as one- and two-bedroom luxury suites.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resource Personnel
Job Location:&nbsp;Berger, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	In charge of all our chains of hotels recruitment need.
	Conduct training and staff development.
	Design compensation and benefits packages.
	Implement performance review procedures (e.g. quarterly/annual and 360&deg; evaluations).
	Develop fair HR policies and ensure employees understand and comply with them.
	Implement effective sourcing, screening and interviewing techniques.
	Assess training needs and coordinate learning and development initiatives for all employees.
	Act as the point of contact regarding labor legislation issues.
	Manage employees&rsquo; grievances.
	Measure employee retention and turnover rates.


Requirements and Skills


	B.Sc / HND in Human Resources Management or any relevant field.
	Female is preferred.
	Proven work experience as an HR Executive or similar role.
	Familiarity with Human Resources Management Systems and Applicant Tracking Systems.
	Experience with full-cycle recruiting.
	Good knowledge of labor legislation (particularly employment contracts, employee leaves and insurance).
	Demonstrable leadership abilities.
	Solid communication skills.
	Hospitality Industry experience is a plus.
	Applicants shouldreside within Ogba, Ikeja, Agege, Ojodu, Ketu axis.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp; hrteam@hotelcapitol.com.ng&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

  "validThrough": "2026-04-01",

  },

Human Resource Personnel Job at Hotel Capitol




        
    

        
        Hotel Capitol is a modern and contemporary hotel that offers not only luxury but comfort at its best. The three-star hotel is strategically located at the heart of Omole Phase 1, making it easily accessible with excellent road networks to other parts of the mainland and the city of Lagos as a whole. The 32-room Hotel offers tastefully furnished deluxe executive and super executive rooms as well as one- and two-bedroom luxury suites.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resource Personnel
Job Location: Berger, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	In charge of all our chains of hotels recruitment need.
	Conduct training and staff development.
	Design compensation and benefits packages.
	Implement performance review procedures (e.g. quarterly/annual and 360° evaluations).
	Develop fair HR policies and ensure employees understand and comply with them.
	Implement effective sourcing, screening and interviewing techniques.
	Assess training needs and coordinate learning and development initiatives for all employees.
	Act as the point of contact regarding labor legislation issues.
	Manage employees’ grievances.
	Measure employee retention and turnover rates.


Requirements and Skills


	B.Sc / HND in Human Resources Management or any relevant field.
	Female is preferred.
	Proven work experience as an HR Executive or similar role.
	Familiarity with Human Resources Management Systems and Applicant Tracking Systems.
	Experience with full-cycle recruiting.
	Good knowledge of labor legislation (particularly employment contracts, employee leaves and insurance).
	Demonstrable leadership abilities.
	Solid communication skills.
	Hospitality Industry experience is a plus.
	Applicants shouldreside within Ogba, Ikeja, Agege, Ojodu, Ketu axis.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:  [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Call Center Executive",
  "description": "Skipper Eye-Q Super Speciality Eye Hospital is an International Eye Hospital chain with the presence of 30 in India and 4 in Nigeria handing all types of Eye Surgeries such as Cataract, Retina, Glaucoma, presbyopia, cornea, squint, refractive error, and other vision problems, and having treated 10+ million patients. Our patients get the same treatment in Nigeria as in the US, UK, and India. We are 18 Years in Eye Care and HMO Empanelled Hospitals

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Call Center Executive
Job Location:&nbsp;Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	A Call Center Executive will act as the crucial first point of contact for patients, responsible formanaging high-volume, empathetic communication, scheduling specialized appointments, and handling patient inquiries regarding eye care services.
	They ensure efficient patient flow and maintain accurate, confidential, and up-to-date patient records.


Key Responsibilities


	Appointment Management:&nbsp;Scheduling, rescheduling, confirming, and managing patient appointments, often using Electronic Medical Record (EMR) or specialized hospital software.
	Patient Interaction &amp; Support:&nbsp;Answering inbound calls and emails to provide information about eye care services, procedures, and doctor schedules.
	Customer Experience &amp; Satisfaction:&nbsp;Handling complaints, de-escalating patient concerns, and conducting patient experience surveys.
	Data Accuracy &amp; Documentation:&nbsp;Accurately recording patient details, inquiries, and complaints into the system.
	Administrative Tasks:&nbsp;Verifying patient demographics, insurance information, and preparing correspondence.
	Clinical Coordination:&nbsp;Collaborating with optometrists, ophthalmologists, and other clinical staff to ensure seamless patient care.
	Follow-Up:&nbsp;Making outbound calls to patients to check on their well-being and satisfaction with services.


Key Requirements


	Education:&nbsp;A Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Social Sciences, Humanities, or Health Information Management is generally required.
	Experience:&nbsp;A minimum of 2-5 years of experience in a similar role is typically required, with experience in a healthcare or medical call center setting being a major advantage.
	Communication Skills:&nbsp;Exceptional verbal and written communication skills in English are essential, with fluency in local languages (e.g., Yoruba, Igbo, Hausa) often required.
	Technical Proficiency:&nbsp;Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint), and familiarity with EMR/hospital software.
	Soft Skills:&nbsp;High level of empathy, patience, ability to work under pressure, attention to detail, and a customer-centric approach.
	Knowledge:&nbsp;Basic understanding of medical or eye care terminology is highly beneficial.
	Compliance:&nbsp;Understanding of patient confidentiality and data protection regulations (e.g., HIPAA).


Key Competencies:


	Multitasking:Ability to manage high-volume calls while navigating systems.
	Problem-Solving:Quick identification of patient issues and resolution.
	Empathy:Ability to remain professional, calm, and supportive to patients, many of whom may be distressed due to vision issues.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their updated CV to:&nbsp;skippereyeqhospitals@gmail.com&nbsp;and copy&nbsp;skippereyeqnigeria@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

  "validThrough": "2026-03-31",

Call Center Executive Job at Skipper Eye-Q Super Speciality Eye Hospital




        
    

        
        Skipper Eye-Q Super Speciality Eye Hospital is an International Eye Hospital chain with the presence of 30 in India and 4 in Nigeria handing all types of Eye Surgeries such as Cataract, Retina, Glaucoma, presbyopia, cornea, squint, refractive error, and other vision problems, and having treated 10+ million patients. Our patients get the same treatment in Nigeria as in the US, UK, and India. We are 18 Years in Eye Care and HMO Empanelled Hospitals

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Call Center Executive
Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	A Call Center Executive will act as the crucial first point of contact for patients, responsible formanaging high-volume, empathetic communication, scheduling specialized appointments, and handling patient inquiries regarding eye care services.
	They ensure efficient patient flow and maintain accurate, confidential, and up-to-date patient records.


Key Responsibilities


	Appointment Management: Scheduling, rescheduling, confirming, and managing patient appointments, often using Electronic Medical Record (EMR) or specialized hospital software.
	Patient Interaction & Support: Answering inbound calls and emails to provide information about eye care services, procedures, and doctor schedules.
	Customer Experience & Satisfaction: Handling complaints, de-escalating patient concerns, and conducting patient experience surveys.
	Data Accuracy & Documentation: Accurately recording patient details, inquiries, and complaints into the system.
	Administrative Tasks: Verifying patient demographics, insurance information, and preparing correspondence.
	Clinical Coordination: Collaborating with optometrists, ophthalmologists, and other clinical staff to ensure seamless patient care.
	Follow-Up: Making outbound calls to patients to check on their well-being and satisfaction with services.


Key Requirements


	Education: A Bachelor's Degree in Social Sciences, Humanities, or Health Information Management is generally required.
	Experience: A minimum of 2-5 years of experience in a similar role is typically required, with experience in a healthcare or medical call center setting being a major advantage.
	Communication Skills: Exceptional verbal and written communication skills in English are essential, with fluency in local languages (e.g., Yoruba, Igbo, Hausa) often required.
	Technical Proficiency: Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint), and familiarity with EMR/hospital software.
	Soft Skills: High level of empathy, patience, ability to work under pressure, attention to detail, and a customer-centric approach.
	Knowledge: Basic understanding of medical or eye care terminology is highly beneficial.
	Compliance: Understanding of patient confidentiality and data protection regulations (e.g., HIPAA).


Key Competencies:


	Multitasking:Ability to manage high-volume calls while navigating systems.
	Problem-Solving:Quick identification of patient issues and resolution.
	Empathy:Ability to remain professional, calm, and supportive to patients, many of whom may be distressed due to vision issues.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their updated CV to: [email protected] and copy [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Billing Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Job Type:&nbsp;Full-Time

Job&nbsp;Description


	The Billing Officer is responsible for managing patient billing processes, ensuring accurate charge capture, timely invoicing, insurance coordination, and efficient revenue collection.
	The role supports transparency, compliance, and a seamless financial experience for patients receiving specialist and neurosurgical care.


Key Responsibilities


	Patient Billing and Invoicing
	Insurance &amp; HMO Processing
	Payment Processign &amp; Reconciliation
	Financial Coordination
	Compliane &amp; Record Keeping


Qualifications &amp; Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree or HND in Accounting, Finance, Business Administration, or related field.
	Minimum of 2 years of experience in hospital billing, preferably in a specialist or tertiary healthcare facility.
	Knowledge of HMO processes and medical billing systems.
	Strong numerical accuracy and attention to detail.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and hospital management software.


Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
	Strong organizational and time-management abilities.
	Integrity and confidentiality.
	Problem-solving and conflict resolution skills.
	Ability to work under pressure in a fast-paced healthcare environment.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;careers@dukesneurosurgerysh.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Billing Officer Job at Dukes Neurosurgery and Specialist Hospital




        
    

        
        Dukes Neurosurgery and Specialist Hospital is a multi-specialist hospital located in the heart of Lagos, Nigeria, dedicated to providing world-class neurosurgical and specialized medical care. Our mission is to provide patient-centered, world-class healthcare services in the field of surgery with the help of well-trained super-specialists and committed staff.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Billing Officer

Job Location: Lagos
Job Type: Full-Time

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Billing Officer is responsible for managing patient billing processes, ensuring accurate charge capture, timely invoicing, insurance coordination, and efficient revenue collection.
	The role supports transparency, compliance, and a seamless financial experience for patients receiving specialist and neurosurgical care.


Key Responsibilities


	Patient Billing and Invoicing
	Insurance & HMO Processing
	Payment Processign & Reconciliation
	Financial Coordination
	Compliane & Record Keeping


Qualifications & Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree or HND in Accounting, Finance, Business Administration, or related field.
	Minimum of 2 years of experience in hospital billing, preferably in a specialist or tertiary healthcare facility.
	Knowledge of HMO processes and medical billing systems.
	Strong numerical accuracy and attention to detail.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office and hospital management software.


Skills & Competencies:


	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
	Strong organizational and time-management abilities.
	Integrity and confidentiality.
	Problem-solving and conflict resolution skills.
	Ability to work under pressure in a fast-paced healthcare environment.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Biomedical Engineer",
  "description": "Skipper Eye-Q Super Speciality Eye Hospital is an International Eye Hospital chain with 30 locations in India and 4 in Nigeria, handling all types of Eye Surgeries such as Cataract, Retina, Glaucoma, presbyopia, cornea, squint, refractive error, and other vision problems,and having treated 10+ million patients. Our patients get the same treatment in Nigeria as in the US, UK, and India. We are 19Years in Eye Care and HMO Empanelled Hospitals

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Biomedical Engineer
Job Location:&nbsp;Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Biomedical engineer will be responsible formanaging, maintaining, and calibrating specialized, high-precision equipment to ensure patient safety and optimal diagnostic/surgical outcomes.
	He/She will act as the technical bridge between medical staff and advanced technology, handling devices such as lasers, microscopes, and imaging systems.


Key Responsibilities


	Maintenance and Repair:&nbsp;Perform Preventive Planned Maintenance (PPM) and corrective repairs on ophthalmic equipment (e.g., Slit Lamps, OCT, Operating Microscopes, Excimer Lasers, Phacoemulsifiers) to ensure minimal downtime.
	Equipment Installation &amp; Calibration:&nbsp;Install new devices, perform pre-acceptance checks, and calibrate equipment to manufacturer specifications.
	Training &amp; Support:&nbsp;Trainon the safe and proper operation of devices.
	Asset Management:&nbsp;Maintain accurate, up-to-date inventories, service histories, and logbooks for all medical equipment.
	Compliance and Safety:&nbsp;Ensure all devices meet regulatory standards (e.g., FDA, ISO 13485) and safety protocols, including conducting electrical safety tests.
	Vendor Management:&nbsp;Monitor service contracts (AMC/CMC) with external suppliers and manage spare parts inventory.


Requirements and Qualifications


	Education:&nbsp;Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Biomedical Engineering, Clinical Engineering, or a closely related field (electronics/electrical engineering).
	Experience:&nbsp;Typically 1&ndash;5 years of experience in a hospital or clinical setting, with specific experience in ophthalmic equipment maintenance highly preferred.
	Technical Skills:&nbsp;Strong knowledge of electronic and mechanical systems, as well as proficiency in using calibration and testing tools.
	Soft Skills:&nbsp;Excellent problem-solving, analytical, and communication skills.
	Requirements:&nbsp;May require the ability to travel between hospital branches and handle emergency service calls.


Typical Eye Hospital Equipment Handled:


	Diagnostic:&nbsp;Optical Coherence Tomography (OCT), Auto Refractometers, Fundus Cameras, Tonometers.
	Surgical:&nbsp;Phacoemulsification units, Operating Microscopes, Femtosecond/Excimer Lasers.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;skippereyeqhospitals@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position&nbsp; as the subject of the email.",

Biomedical Engineer Job at Skipper Eye-Q Super Speciality Eye Hospital




        
    

        
        Skipper Eye-Q Super Speciality Eye Hospital is an International Eye Hospital chain with 30 locations in India and 4 in Nigeria, handling all types of Eye Surgeries such as Cataract, Retina, Glaucoma, presbyopia, cornea, squint, refractive error, and other vision problems,and having treated 10+ million patients. Our patients get the same treatment in Nigeria as in the US, UK, and India. We are 19Years in Eye Care and HMO Empanelled Hospitals

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Biomedical Engineer
Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Biomedical engineer will be responsible formanaging, maintaining, and calibrating specialized, high-precision equipment to ensure patient safety and optimal diagnostic/surgical outcomes.
	He/She will act as the technical bridge between medical staff and advanced technology, handling devices such as lasers, microscopes, and imaging systems.


Key Responsibilities


	Maintenance and Repair: Perform Preventive Planned Maintenance (PPM) and corrective repairs on ophthalmic equipment (e.g., Slit Lamps, OCT, Operating Microscopes, Excimer Lasers, Phacoemulsifiers) to ensure minimal downtime.
	Equipment Installation & Calibration: Install new devices, perform pre-acceptance checks, and calibrate equipment to manufacturer specifications.
	Training & Support: Trainon the safe and proper operation of devices.
	Asset Management: Maintain accurate, up-to-date inventories, service histories, and logbooks for all medical equipment.
	Compliance and Safety: Ensure all devices meet regulatory standards (e.g., FDA, ISO 13485) and safety protocols, including conducting electrical safety tests.
	Vendor Management: Monitor service contracts (AMC/CMC) with external suppliers and manage spare parts inventory.


Requirements and Qualifications


	Education: Bachelor’s degree in Biomedical Engineering, Clinical Engineering, or a closely related field (electronics/electrical engineering).
	Experience: Typically 1–5 years of experience in a hospital or clinical setting, with specific experience in ophthalmic equipment maintenance highly preferred.
	Technical Skills: Strong knowledge of electronic and mechanical systems, as well as proficiency in using calibration and testing tools.
	Soft Skills: Excellent problem-solving, analytical, and communication skills.
	Requirements: May require the ability to travel between hospital branches and handle emergency service calls.


Typical Eye Hospital Equipment Handled:


	Diagnostic: Optical Coherence Tomography (OCT), Auto Refractometers, Fundus Cameras, Tonometers.
	Surgical: Phacoemulsification units, Operating Microscopes, Femtosecond/Excimer Lasers.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position  as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

Job Position: Operations Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Garki, Abuja (FCT)&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Description


	The Operations Manager will provide strategic and operational leadership for&nbsp;OneVERIFY&rsquo;s business operations, ensuring seamless coordination across teams,&nbsp;projects, and technology platforms.
	The ideal candidate will be proactive, highly&nbsp;organized, and capable of translating strategic objectives into actionable operational&nbsp;plans.
	This role will oversee internal processes, vendor partnerships, and project&nbsp;execution while ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements and industry&nbsp;standards.


Key Responsibilities
Operational Leadership:


	Manage day-to-day business operations, ensuring efficiency and&nbsp;alignment with organizational goals.
	Develop, implement, and monitor operational strategies, processes,&nbsp;and procedures to optimize performance across teams.
	Lead cross-functional initiatives to enhance workflow, productivity, and&nbsp;customer satisfaction.


Project &amp; Program Management:


	Oversee the planning, execution, and delivery of technology-driven&nbsp;projects, including OneV erify deployments.
	Coordinate with product, engineering, and support teams to ensure&nbsp;milestones are met and quality standards maintained.
	Implement risk management strategies to identify, mitigate, and&nbsp;escalate operational challenges.


People &amp; Team Management:


	Supervise and mentor operations staff, fostering a culture of&nbsp;accountability, efficiency, and continuous improvement.
	Set performance goals, monitor KPIs, and conduct regular evaluations.
	Promote knowledge sharing and ensure effective collaboration across&nbsp;remote and hybrid teams.


Process Optimization &amp; Compliance:


	Analyze existing workflows and recommend improvements for&nbsp;operational scalability.
	Ensure compliance with regulatory frameworks, data privacy laws, and&nbsp;internal policies.
	Develop standard operating procedures (SOPs) and operational&nbsp;manuals for ongoing reference.


Vendor &amp; Stakeholder Management:


	Manage relationships with external vendors, service providers, and&nbsp;government partners.
	Negotiate contracts, monitor service-level agreements, and ensure&nbsp;cost-effective operations.
	Serve as a liaison between senior management and operational teams,&nbsp;providing reports and insights to guide decision-making.


Reporting &amp; Analytics:


	Maintain dashboards, performance metrics, and operational reports for&nbsp;executive leadership.
	Conduct data-driven analysis to inform strategic planning and&nbsp;operational improvements.
	Track progress of initiatives and recommend adjustments to improve&nbsp;efficiency and outcomes.


Required Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Business Administration, Operations Management,&nbsp;Engineering, or a related field (Master&rsquo;s preferred).
	Minimum of 7 years of experience in operations management within technology,&nbsp;SaaS, or fintech companies.
	Strong understanding of digital identity platforms, RegTech, and technology-&nbsp;driven operational workflows.
	Excellent project management, analytical, and organizational skills.
	Proficiency in operational software, dashboards, reporting tools, and&nbsp;collaboration platforms.
	Exceptional leadership, interpersonal, and problem-solving abilities.


Method of Application&nbsp;

Operations Manager Job at OneVERIFY Limited




        
    

        
        OneVERIFY Limited is a leading Nigerian technology company specializing in digital identity, verification, RegTech, AI, cybersecurity, and blockchain-based solutions. We build platforms that enable secure identity verification, AML/KYC compliance, fraud prevention, and data integrity for both government and private sector clients. Our people are our greatest asset, and we prioritize fostering a high-performance, inclusive, and compliant organizational culture.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Operations Manager

Job Location: Garki, Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time

Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Operations Manager will provide strategic and operational leadership for OneVERIFY’s business operations, ensuring seamless coordination across teams, projects, and technology platforms.
	The ideal candidate will be proactive, highly organized, and capable of translating strategic objectives into actionable operational plans.
	This role will oversee internal processes, vendor partnerships, and project execution while ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements and industry standards.


Key Responsibilities
Operational Leadership:


	Manage day-to-day business operations, ensuring efficiency and alignment with organizational goals.
	Develop, implement, and monitor operational strategies, processes, and procedures to optimize performance across teams.
	Lead cross-functional initiatives to enhance workflow, productivity, and customer satisfaction.


Project & Program Management:


	Oversee the planning, execution, and delivery of technology-driven projects, including OneV erify deployments.
	Coordinate with product, engineering, and support teams to ensure milestones are met and quality standards maintained.
	Implement risk management strategies to identify, mitigate, and escalate operational challenges.


People & Team Management:


	Supervise and mentor operations staff, fostering a culture of accountability, efficiency, and continuous improvement.
	Set performance goals, monitor KPIs, and conduct regular evaluations.
	Promote knowledge sharing and ensure effective collaboration across remote and hybrid teams.


Process Optimization & Compliance:


	Analyze existing workflows and recommend improvements for operational scalability.
	Ensure compliance with regulatory frameworks, data privacy laws, and internal policies.
	Develop standard operating procedures (SOPs) and operational manuals for ongoing reference.


Vendor & Stakeholder Management:


	Manage relationships with external vendors, service providers, and government partners.
	Negotiate contracts, monitor service-level agreements, and ensure cost-effective operations.
	Serve as a liaison between senior management and operational teams, providing reports and insights to guide decision-making.


Reporting & Analytics:


	Maintain dashboards, performance metrics, and operational reports for executive leadership.
	Conduct data-driven analysis to inform strategic planning and operational improvements.
	Track progress of initiatives and recommend adjustments to improve efficiency and outcomes.


Required Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration, Operations Management, Engineering, or a related field (Master’s preferred).
	Minimum of 7 years of experience in operations management within technology, SaaS, or fintech companies.
	Strong understanding of digital identity platforms, RegTech, and technology- driven operational workflows.
	Excellent project management, analytical, and organizational skills.
	Proficiency in operational software, dashboards, reporting tools, and collaboration platforms.
	Exceptional leadership, interpersonal, and problem-solving abilities.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV / Cover Letter to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.
